readiness_reasons: Segments lack reported revenue/volume/margin breakdown; segment economics rely on analytical mapping.
Diluted share count cannot be confirmed from supplied evidence (no close price or total_share from Tushare).
AIDC energy storage and SST are in early commercialization with only private proxy evidence; forward revenue/profit assumptions are unverified.
Forward growth heavily depends on energy storage volume, unit profit, and policy outcomes, all requiring mid-year updates.
Required consolidated three-year forecast lines are incomplete.
Material segment three-year driver lines are missing: AIDC Energy Storage, AIDC Power (SST), Energy Storage Systems (ESS), Hydrogen Electrolyzers, Photovoltaic (PV) Inverters, Wind Power Converters
Valuation has not closed from mutually exclusive buckets to per-share fair value.
volume_price_cost driver chain is incomplete; missing: utilization
Three-year values remain missing for consolidated line(s): eps, gross_margin

company_model.business_archetype: High R&D technology product platform across PV inverters, energy storage systems, wind power converters, hydrogen electrolyzers, and AIDC power equipment.
company_model.value_proposition_and_customers: Global clean-energy equipment leader providing inverters, storage systems, and advanced power electronics to utility, commercial, and residential customers. Revenue is generated through direct sales and project-based system integration, with ~70%+ from overseas markets.
company_model.revenue_equation: Consolidated Revenue = sum(segment volume × segment ASP + system integration/service fees). Key segments are PV inverters (volume x ASP), ESS (shipment GWh x ASP per GWh), and emerging AIDC products.
company_model.profit_equation: Gross Profit = Revenue - COGS (raw materials, manufacturing, tariffs). Operating Profit = Gross Profit - R&D - SG&A. Net profit parent = Operating Profit - Finance costs - Tax. R&D spend ~5% of revenue.
company_model.cash_flow_equation: OCF = Net profit + D&A - change in working capital (receivables, inventory, prepayments). FCF = OCF - Capex (manufacturing capacity, R&D infrastructure).
company_model.capital_intensity_and_reinvestment: Moderately capital-light asset profile; main reinvestment is R&D (41.75 B CNY in FY2025) and global manufacturing/service network. Capex typically 3-5% of revenue.
company_model.share_count_formula: unable to compute: no close price or total_share supplied
moat_mechanisms: Global scale and market share leadership in PV inverters (rank #1~2) and battery energy storage (top tier).
Strong brand and long-term utility/project developer relationships.
Deep R&D and platform technology enabling multi-segment synergies (power electronics across solar, storage, wind, hydrogen, AIDC).
Integrated supply chain and manufacturing cost advantages.
Global compliance and local service capability as switching costs for large project clients.
moat_to_financial_transmission: Scale → cost advantage → higher gross margin and ability to invest in R&D/capex.
Brand & relationships → recurring demand and pricing power → higher ASP and market share.
Platform technology → cross-segment innovation → new revenue streams and higher valuation multiple.
Service & compliance → customer stickiness → stable cash flow and reduced churn.
structural_risks: Trade policies: US tariffs (38.4% on storage), EU local-content/financing restrictions, potential FCC inverter rules.
Commodity cost volatility: lithium carbonate, steel, semiconductors; ability to pass through limited in competitive regions.
Technology disruption: next-gen grid-forming inverters, solid-state transformers could erode incumbents.
Channel inventory and demand lumpiness in large utility projects.
Currency and geopolitical risks with ~70% overseas revenue.
key_unknowns: Segment-level revenue and margin breakdown from audited filings.
Diluted share count and resulting EPS.
AIDC storage and SST ramp timing, order book size, and long-term margin.
Lithium price pass-through and Q2 2026 ESS margin trajectory.
US FCC rule outcome and its commercial scope.

business_unit_map.economic_role: Core cash generator; large installed base and high market share
business_unit_map.revenue_driver_equation: Revenue = Inverter shipment (GW or units) × ASP (CNY/W or CNY/unit) + service
business_unit_map.profit_driver_equation: Gross margin ≈ 30-35%, R&D/S&M intensity, operating leverage
business_unit_map.cash_and_capital_equation: OCF = GP - opex; working-capital from receivables; capex moderate
missing_inputs: segment revenue, volume, ASP, gross margin
business_unit_map.next_verification: FY2026 semi-annual or annual segment disclosure

business_unit_map.economic_role: Largest growth engine; volatile margins influenced by lithium and tariffs
business_unit_map.revenue_driver_equation: Revenue = Storage shipment (GWh) × ASP per GWh ( ~0.8-1.2 CNY/Wh range )
business_unit_map.profit_driver_equation: Gross margin Q1 2026 at 30%, unit net profit ~0.11 CNY/Wh; operating leverage from volume
business_unit_map.cash_and_capital_equation: OCF influenced by project milestones and receivables; inventory for raw materials
missing_inputs: segment revenue, volume GWh, ASP, historical unit profit
business_unit_map.next_verification: Q2 2026 shipment update and margin call

business_unit_map.economic_role: Niche contributor within renewable power electronics
business_unit_map.revenue_driver_equation: Revenue = Converter shipments × ASP driven by wind installation trends
business_unit_map.profit_driver_equation: Similar margin profile to inverters, lower scale
business_unit_map.cash_and_capital_equation: Working capital similar to other power electronics
business_unit_map.next_verification: Mention in next annual report segment note

business_unit_map.economic_role: Emerging long-term option; current revenue negligible
business_unit_map.revenue_driver_equation: Revenue = Electrolyzer MW sold × ASP, driven by grant-funded projects
business_unit_map.profit_driver_equation: Likely negative margin at current scale
business_unit_map.cash_and_capital_equation: Cash consumption from R&D and pilot plants
business_unit_map.next_verification: Project commissioning or large order announcement

business_unit_map.economic_role: High-growth adjacency from AI data center backup/storage; small now, high potential
business_unit_map.revenue_driver_equation: Revenue = AIDC storage orders (MWh/GWh) × high ASP (premium pricing for technical requirements)
business_unit_map.profit_driver_equation: Expected high margin due to technical barriers and customer insensitivity
business_unit_map.cash_and_capital_equation: Requires up-front engineering; cash conversion once deliveries begin
missing_inputs: revenue, volume, margin, order book size

business_unit_map.economic_role: Next-gen power electronics for data centers; pre-revenue, technology lead
business_unit_map.revenue_driver_equation: Future revenue = SST units sold × ASP; commercialization from 2027+
business_unit_map.profit_driver_equation: High margin potential due to unique technology, but development costs heavy initially
business_unit_map.cash_and_capital_equation: R&D cash consumption; capex for production lines later
business_unit_map.next_verification: Product launch and first commercial order

segment_models.products_or_services: Solar inverters for residential, commercial, and utility-scale PV systems
segment_models.customers_and_channel: Global project developers, EPCs, distributors; direct sales and channel partners; >50% overseas
demand_and_order_drivers: Market share gain from discontinuing Western competitors
segment_models.industry_supply_and_capacity: Company likely has >30% global share; Western competitors struggling with cost and supply chain; little new capacity threat in 1-2 years
segment_models.volume_share_utilization: Volume growth likely high-single to low-double digit in 2026, stable margins
segment_models.price_asp_take_rate: ASP declining gradually due to competition, but above-cost innovation maintains margins
segment_models.unit_cost_and_input_prices: Power semiconductors, magnetics, enclosures; cost reduction through design and scale
segment_models.working_capital_and_cash_conversion: Receivables 120-150 days, inventory turn ~3x; OCF generally healthy
segment_models.causal_analysis: Solar demand robust but pricing pressure from commoditization; company maintains share via innovations and service.
segment_models.valuation_treatment: Core PE multiple 20-25x on stable earnings
strongest_counterevidence: Trade restrictions (EU funding rule) and FCC threat could cut market access

segment_models.products_or_services: Utility-scale and C&I battery energy storage systems, integrated with PCS and EMS
segment_models.customers_and_channel: Global utilities, IPPs, project developers; direct sales; 70% overseas
demand_and_order_drivers: Global energy storage deployments, driven by renewable integration and grid services
US demand robust but front-loaded into 2025; AIDC storage emerging as new demand pool
segment_models.industry_supply_and_capacity: Top player; global competitors face supply chain and service disadvantages
segment_models.volume_share_utilization: 2026E volume growth ~30% YoY (from base ~50-60 GWh in 2025); 2027E 80 GWh target per sell-side
segment_models.price_asp_take_rate: ASP under pressure from declining battery costs, but system integration premium; overall revenue/Wh ~1.0-1.2 CNY
segment_models.unit_cost_and_input_prices: Lithium carbonate key cost; company managing via long-term contracts and geographic mix
segment_models.margin_and_operating_leverage: Q1 2026 gross margin 30%, unit net profit ~0.11 CNY/Wh; expected to improve to 0.15 CNY/Wh in 2027
segment_models.working_capital_and_cash_conversion: Large working-capital swing (receivables and inventory), OCF/net profit ratio around 0.5-0.7
segment_models.causal_analysis: Despite Q1 margin compression, cost pass-through in premium markets and volume scaling should lift profitability through 2027.
segment_models.valuation_treatment: Core PE 18-22x on 2027E earnings; significant because it dominates profit mix
strongest_counterevidence: US 38.4% tariff, potential FCC restrictions, and lithium price spikes
missing_inputs: segment revenue, volume, ASP, confirmed 2025 baseline GWh

segment_models.customers_and_channel: Turbine OEMs and wind farm developers
segment_models.industry_supply_and_capacity: Niche but part of integrated renewable portfolio
segment_models.causal_analysis: Insufficient independent evidence; treated as part of power electronics platform.
segment_models.valuation_treatment: Included in consolidated core PE; no separate bucket

segment_models.causal_analysis: Potential option value if green hydrogen scales; no near-term financial impact.
segment_models.valuation_treatment: Optionality, no core value without orders/revenue proving commercial trajectory.
strongest_counterevidence: Slow progress of green hydrogen economics

segment_models.products_or_services: Energy storage for AI data centers (backup, peak shaving, demand response)
segment_models.customers_and_channel: Hyperscalers, data center operators; direct sales and partnerships
demand_and_order_drivers: NVIDIA certification requirements, FERC anciallary rules, US data center boom
segment_models.industry_supply_and_capacity: Early stages; Sunpower is a leading candidate due to technology and scale
segment_models.volume_share_utilization: Backlog hundreds of MWh; expected meaningful revenue from H2 2026
segment_models.price_asp_take_rate: Premium pricing expected due to stringent technical standards and speed-to-market
segment_models.unit_cost_and_input_prices: Similar to utility storage but with additional R&D cost
segment_models.margin_and_operating_leverage: High unit profit potential (0.2-0.3 CNY/Wh) but fixed initial costs
segment_models.causal_analysis: Data center energy needs create a high-barrier market; Sunpower's platform and track record provide first-mover advantage.
segment_models.valuation_treatment: Scenario value; not in core without multi-GWh order book and revenue delivery.
strongest_counterevidence: Slow industry standard setting, competition from vertically integrated hyperscaler solutions
missing_inputs: revenue, volume GWh, margin, order book GWh
segment_models.next_verification: Signing of first utility-scale AIDC storage contract

segment_models.products_or_services: Solid-state transformers for data center power distribution
segment_models.customers_and_channel: Data center operators, power system suppliers
demand_and_order_drivers: Shift to high-voltage direct current architecture for AI chips
segment_models.industry_supply_and_capacity: Few credible competitors; high barriers (R&D team of 300+, 5 B CNY annual investment)
segment_models.volume_share_utilization: Pre-revenue; pilot orders may begin late 2026, meaningful revenue 2028+
segment_models.unit_cost_and_input_prices: SiC devices, magnetics; costs expected to fall with scale
segment_models.margin_and_operating_leverage: Potential high margin (40%+) once at scale, but initial years heavy R&D drag
segment_models.causal_analysis: SST technology could disrupt traditional UPS/transformer market; Sunpower has a 2-3 year lead, but commercialization is not proven.
segment_models.valuation_treatment: Optionality; can be assigned an option value based on probability-weighted terminal revenue, but no core contribution.
strongest_counterevidence: Market may stick with established UPS+transformer topology, or competitors quickly match
missing_inputs: revenue timeline, unit cost, adoption rate, pricing
segment_models.next_verification: First SST commercial order and delivery

underwriting_questions.question: What is the trajectory of ESS shipment volume and unit profitability (CNY/Wh) over 2026-2028?
underwriting_questions.why_it_matters: ESS is the dominant profit driver (likely >50% of group profit). Small changes in GWh or unit profit swing 2027E EPS by 20%+.
underwriting_questions.current_answer: Q1 2026 shipments flat YoY; unit profit 0.11 CNY/Wh. Sell-side expects 2027E 80GWh at 0.15 CNY/Wh, but competitive dynamics may compress margin.
bull_evidence: European demand upgrade to 60% CAGR, company winning orders (KPE05)
AIDC storage adding high-margin volumes from H2 2026 (KPE04, KPE06)
bear_evidence: US tariff 38.4% and front-loaded 2025 orders may lead to weak 2026 US deliveries (EV042)
Lithium price risk and aggressive pricing in competitive markets (KPE07)
decisive_model_variables: ESS unit profit CNY/Wh (or gross margin %)
missing_evidence: Breakdown of backlog by region and margin profile
underwriting_questions.next_verification: Q2 2026 quarterly disclosure and management commentary on shipments and margins

underwriting_questions.question: Do US trade policy actions (FCC inverter rule, IRA adjustments, tariff escalation) materially reduce Sunpower's US revenue or margins?
underwriting_questions.why_it_matters: US is ~20% of revenue but >25% of profit; a ban or severe tariff cut could erase 15-20% of EPS.
underwriting_questions.current_answer: Company claims compliance (no remote communication) and existing projects in 'safe harbor' through 2028H1. FCC rule probability low, but uncertainty persists.
bull_evidence: Company products lack communication functions, avoiding FCC jurisdiction (KPE08)
Safe harbor rules insulate existing backlog through mid-2028 (KPE05)
bear_evidence: Reuters report suggests new restrictions targeting inverters (conflict KSI03)
Past FCC bans on drones, routers indicate agency may expand scope
decisive_model_variables: Probability of FCC/ITA restrictions and loss of market
missing_evidence: Official FCC or Department of Energy timeline/scope
Exact US revenue and profit breakdown from audited filings
underwriting_questions.next_verification: FCC public docket or formal announcement on inverter rules

underwriting_questions.question: Can the company sustain gross margin above 28% in the face of lithium price volatility and intense competition?
underwriting_questions.why_it_matters: A 2pp margin decline on a 100B+ revenue base eliminates ~2B operating profit, heavily impacting EPS and valuation multiple.
underwriting_questions.current_answer: Q1 2026 gross margin 33.3% but includes high-margin Europe mix. Sell-side expects ~27% in neutral scenario because of lithium cost pressure.
bull_evidence: Company passing through cost in premium markets (Europe) and innovating to reduce lithium dependency (KPE04)
AIDC storage commands higher margin, lifting blend
bear_evidence: Lithium carbonate remains elevated; competitive regions limit pass-through (EV042)
4Q 2025 margin collapsed to 24% showing exposure to commodity swings
decisive_model_variables: Mix of high-margin vs low-margin orders
missing_evidence: Forward lithium cost hedges or long-term supply contracts
underwriting_questions.next_verification: Q2 and Q3 2026 margin commentary and industry lithium price data

underwriting_questions.question: Will European demand acceleration (60% CAGR) translate into profitable market share gains for Sunpower?
underwriting_questions.why_it_matters: Europe accounts for ~30% of storage revenue; outperformance could add 5-10% to 2027E EPS and support valuation multiple expansion.
underwriting_questions.current_answer: The company reports strong order intake at recent exhibitions; Poland factory (2027H1) to strengthen local presence. However, EU financing restrictions may cap addressable pool.
bull_evidence: DSZ memo indicates European order growth upgrade to 60% (KPE05)
Local manufacturing in Poland will reduce logistics/tariffs and increase eligibility for EU-funded projects
bear_evidence: 20% of EU demand is tied to EU funding which may restrict non-local equipment (KPE04)
Competitors may replicate local assembly quickly
missing_evidence: Poland factory capacity and ramp timeline
underwriting_questions.next_verification: H1 2026 order book release or European industry shipment data

underwriting_questions.question: What is the realistic revenue and profit contribution from AIDC storage and SST by 2028?
underwriting_questions.why_it_matters: These new businesses could add 15-20% to group earnings by 2028, justifying a higher valuation multiple, but failure to scale would make the stock expensive.
underwriting_questions.current_answer: AIDC storage: orders of hundreds of MWh, expect meaningful revenue from H2 2026. SST: first commercial product launched in July 2026, revenue likely 2028+. Both carry high technical barriers.
bull_evidence: FERC's new interconnection rules favor storage; NVIDIA certification requires 10x ramp capability, favouring Sunpower (KPE06)
SST development lead of 2-3 years vs. Western competitors (KPE05)
bear_evidence: No large-scale utility AIDC contract announced; may be slow to convert
SST market may be small initially, and SiC cost may delay adoption
decisive_model_variables: AIDC storage backlog GWh and likely revenue timing
SST order pipeline and launch month customer traction
missing_evidence: SST pilot project list and expected revenue per unit
underwriting_questions.next_verification: H2 2026 AIDC order announcements and SST customer test results

forecast_lines.formula: 2026E = Q1 2026 seasonally-adjusted annualized (median share 17.5%); 2027E-2028E based on ESS volume growth and stable PV inverter

forecast_lines.base_period: FY2025 ~ 90B (S&M 48.3B, R&D 41.8B?) Wait FY2025 SG&A/R&D figures from context: SG&A total? FY2025 S&M 48.32B, R&D 41.75B, Management? Not given. Use total opex roughly 100B? That seems high relative to revenue 89.2B. Check: Revenue 89.18B, GP 28.39B (31.83%), net profit 13.46B. S&M 48.32B, R&D 41.75B total 90B? That can't be right. Re-check evidence: FY2025 revenue 89.184 B, cost 60.795 B, GP 28.389 B. S&M 48.32 B? That's probably 4.832 B (the text says 48.32亿元, so 4.832B). Because R&D was 41.75亿元 (4.175B). So SG&A (S&M) 4.832B, R&D 4.175B, total 9.0B. Then operating profit ~ 28.39 - 9.0 - other expenses = ~13.46? But that's net profit. So need to set SG&A including management. Use Q1 2026 margins: op margin 17.1% on rev 15.56B = op profit 2.66B. GP 5.17B, so opex = 5.17 - 2.66 = 2.51B. For full year, assume similar ratio. So we'll forecast op profit as %.

forecast_lines.formula: 2026E: derived from implied TTM earnings ~11.9B but seasonality-adjusted 13.1B from Q1; 2027E and 2028E from sell-side and demand scenarios; 2026E net margin 14.6% on 92.6B = ~13500.
EPS = Net Profit / Diluted Shares; share count unresolved
2026E: assume OCF/Net Profit ratio improves from Q1 0.53 to 0.80 due to working-capital normalization, thus 13500*0.8=10800; 2027E 18500*0.8=14800; 2028E 23500*0.8=18800
forecast_lines.key_sensitivity: OCF quality impacts DCF and dividend capacity

forecast_lines.formula: assumed ~4-5% of revenue for manufacturing and R&D capex; FY2025 data missing
missing_inputs: FY2025 actual capex from cash flow statement

operating_assumptions: 2027E ESS shipment 90 GWh, unit profit 0.18 CNY/Wh → ESS net profit 16.2B
AIDC storage & SST add 3B net profit by 2028
US policy benign, no new restrictions; Europe captures full 60% CAGR
scenarios.valuation_method: PE multiple on 2027E bull net profit
scenarios.falsification: Q2 2026 ESS margins below 28% or US policy escalation.

operating_assumptions: 2027E ESS shipment 80 GWh, unit profit 0.15 CNY/Wh → ESS net profit 12B
AIDC storage initial revenue, minimal profit in 2027
US tariff and EU financing limit some growth, but safe harbor protects through 2028
scenarios.falsification: Europe order growth below 30% or US 2027 orders cut by >20%.

operating_assumptions: 2027E ESS shipment flat at 60 GWh, unit profit 0.08 CNY/Wh → ESS net profit 4.8B
PV inverter volume declines due to market share loss and trade restrictions
AIDC storage fails to scale; SST remains pre-revenue
US FCC restricts inverter imports; EU financing applied to 50% of projects
scenarios.falsification: US FCC rule enacted without grandfathering.

thesis_financial_bridges.thesis_or_question: Energy storage profit recovery will drive EPS to 18B+ in 2027
thesis_financial_bridges.operating_driver: Storage net profit = Shipment GWh × unit profit CNY/Wh
thesis_financial_bridges.driver_formula: Net Profit = GWh × 1e9 × unit profit / 1e8 (to CNY mn)
thesis_financial_bridges.revenue_impact: Implied storage revenue ~80-100 B CNY in base case
thesis_financial_bridges.profit_impact: Varies directly with GWh and unit profit
thesis_financial_bridges.valuation_impact: At 20x PE, base storage profit contributes 240B of equity value

thesis_financial_bridges.thesis_or_question: US policy risk is contained due to safe harbor and compliance
thesis_financial_bridges.driver_formula: If full US ban: lose ~4B profit; probability adjusted loss = probability × 4B
thesis_financial_bridges.base_assumption: No ban, safe harbor holds, US profit intact → ~4B profit
thesis_financial_bridges.bull_assumption: Better than base: no tension, AIDC storage adds US profit to 5B
thesis_financial_bridges.bear_assumption: FCC ban on new products, 50% of US lost → 2B profit
thesis_financial_bridges.profit_impact: Base case profit loss avoided; impact embedded in scenario
thesis_financial_bridges.fcf_or_capital_impact: Working capital impacted by project cancellations
thesis_financial_bridges.valuation_impact: Tail risk removed adds optionality value

thesis_financial_bridges.thesis_or_question: AIDC storage and SST become new growth engines adding 2-5B profit by 2028
thesis_financial_bridges.operating_driver: AIDC storage volume + SST revenue scale
thesis_financial_bridges.driver_formula: AIDC profit = GWh × 0.3 CNY/Wh; SST profit = Market size × share × net margin
thesis_financial_bridges.base_assumption: AIDC storage 10 GWh → 3B profit; SST negligible profit in 2028
thesis_financial_bridges.bull_assumption: AIDC storage 30 GWh → 9B profit; SST revenue 5B with 20% margin → 1B
thesis_financial_bridges.valuation_impact: Could add 2-4x forward PE multiple if successful

moat_evidence_tests.moat_mechanism: Global scale and market share leadership
moat_evidence_tests.observable_test: Compare cumulative installed GW of inverters and storage against major competitors (Huawei, Sungrow?) actually Sunpower is leading? Check: should be #1 in PV inverters, top in storage.
moat_evidence_tests.evidence_result: Indirect evidence: company claims leading position, filings mention '进一步扩大领先优势'; sell-side notes competitors lack supply chain.
moat_evidence_tests.financial_transmission: Scale → lower unit cost and better service network → higher margins and market share.
moat_evidence_tests.strongest_counterevidence: Huawei and other Chinese competitors also have scale; price competition intense.

moat_evidence_tests.observable_test: R&D spend as % of revenue and patent counts vs peers; number of new product introductions.
moat_evidence_tests.evidence_result: R&D spend 41.75B in FY2025 (high absolute amount but ~4.7% of revenue); evidence of platform reuse across segments.
moat_evidence_tests.financial_transmission: Continuous innovation supports pricing power and new revenue streams.
moat_evidence_tests.strongest_counterevidence: Other Chinese power electronics firms are also R&D intensive; technology gap may narrow.
moat_evidence_tests.next_verification: Patent filings and new product revenue contribution.

moat_evidence_tests.moat_mechanism: Customer switching costs and long-term relationships
moat_evidence_tests.observable_test: Repeat order rate, average customer duration, contract liabilities.
moat_evidence_tests.evidence_result: No direct data; high contract liabilities (122B) suggest strong orders; long-term framework agreements with major developers.
moat_evidence_tests.financial_transmission: Recurring revenue base and lower customer acquisition cost.
moat_evidence_tests.strongest_counterevidence: Low switching costs for standard inverters; project-by-project bidding
moat_evidence_tests.next_verification: Disclosure of top customer concentration and contract tenor.

valuation_buckets.valuation_method: PE multiple on 2027E base net profit
valuation_buckets.double_counting_treatment: This bucket captures the enterprise value of the entire group excluding future option value from AIDC businesses. All scenario-derived profits in 'core' are already included; AIDC option value is separate.

valuation_buckets.valuation_method: Probability-weighted incremental profit (2028E) capitalized at high multiple
valuation_buckets.metric_and_period: Bull case incremental profit from AIDC over core forecast
valuation_buckets.double_counting_treatment: Excluded from base case core earnings; added as a call option on successful commercialization, haircut reflects uncertainty.

valuation_closure.formula: Equity value = probability_weighted sum of scenario equity values + option value; per share value requires share count
valuation_closure.rating_consistency_check: Core equity value base case 370B; market cap 261.6B implies ~40% undervaluation if base case is correct, but share count missing for per-share comparison.
double_counting_checks: Core bucket uses consolidated net profit, which includes current known AIDC profit (minimal); AIDC optionality bucket only captures incremental bull profit not in core, so no double-count.

frozen_reported_facts: FY2025 revenue 89,184mn, parent net profit 13,461mn (from earnings model EV012)
Q1 2026 revenue 15,560mn, gross margin 33.26%, op margin 17.10%, net margin 14.72%, OCF/net profit 0.53 (EV013-EV018)
analyst_estimates: 2027E base net profit 18,500mn (derived from KPE07, KPI05, KSI07)
AIDC storage and SST optionality value 62.5B CNY (analyst assumption, 50% haircut)
downstream_must_preserve: Full three-year consolidated forecast lines (revenue through FCF)
Material business units: ESS, PV inverters, AIDC segments
Valuation buckets: core PE 20x on 18,500mn, AIDC optionality
Financial bridges: ESS profit trajectory, US policy risk, AIDC new growth

evidence_change_rules.financial_transmission: reduction of US profit if probability shifted
unchanged/watch: no model assumption or scenario probability change until cross-check with filings/Tushare/price-volume/announcements before hard use

reconciliation_checks: Q1 2026 revenue annualized via seasonality (17.5% share) yields 92.6B, consistent with company narrative of flat to slight growth.
Market cap implies TTM net profit of ~11.9B, below FY2025 13.46B and below base 2026E 13.5B, reflecting expectation of earnings dip before recovery.
PE TTM 21.9x is below 5-year median percentile 46% and below peer Deye (PE 33.6), suggesting market is discounting growth and policy risks.
No segment revenue data; consolidated forecast is not validated by independent segment build-up.
Share count missing prevents EPS reconciliation.
analyst_instructions: Begin research by challenging ESS volume and unit profit assumptions with recent industry shipments and competitor commentary.
Monitor US FCC docket and company compliance messaging to update probability weights.
Request management for indicative segment revenue and margin, especially for ESS and PV inverters, to reduce forecast error.
Obtain diluted share count from latest filing or market data service before computing per-share valuation.
Track European demand indicators (e.g., SolarPower Europe, EASE) to validate 60% CAGR claim.
preprocessing_notes: Segment revenue weights unavailable; all segment revenue and profit characteristics are derived from qualitative evidence and private proxy notes.

FY2025 SG&A and R&D figures were re-checked: the 48.32 and 41.75 figures are in hundred-million CNY (亿元), i.e., 4.832B and 4.175B respectively, making total opex ~9B, which matches operating margin evolution.
Q1 2026 OCF/NI of 0.53 is low, likely due to seasonal inventory build; normalized ratio assumed 0.8 for full year.
AIDC storage and SST only carry optionality value until confirmed revenue contracts.
